formulas.name: Berne Convention
formulas.formula: The Berne Convention is an international agreement that protects the intellectual property rights of creators
formulas.notes: This is a key international treaty for protecting copyrights

formulas.name: Copyright Law
formulas.formula: Copyright protects the original literary, dramatic, musical and artistic works, including software, from unauthorized use
formulas.notes: This law gives exclusive rights to creators for their work

formulas.name: Patent Law
formulas.formula: A patent is a grant of exclusive rights to an inventor for a limited time to make, use, and sell an invention
formulas.notes: This is a key law for protecting inventions and innovations

formulas.name: Trademark Law
formulas.formula: A trademark is a distinctive sign or symbol that identifies a product or service and distinguishes it from others
formulas.notes: This law gives exclusive rights to businesses and individuals for their brand identities

formulas.name: Industrial Design Law
formulas.formula: Industrial design refers to the ornamental or aesthetic aspects of a product, such as its shape, configuration, or pattern
formulas.notes: This law protects the visual appearance of products

formulas.name: Geographical Indication Law
formulas.formula: Geographical indication refers to a sign or symbol that indicates the geographical origin of a product
formulas.notes: This law protects the names of places associated with products

formulas.name: Copyright Infringement
formulas.formula: Copyright infringement occurs when someone uses, reproduces, or distributes a copyrighted work without permission
formulas.notes: This is a key concept in copyright law

formulas.name: Patent Infringement
formulas.formula: Patent infringement occurs when someone makes, uses, or sells a patented invention without permission
formulas.notes: This is a key concept in patent law

formulas.name: Trademark Infringement
formulas.formula: Trademark infringement occurs when someone uses a trademark that is similar to an existing trademark and causes confusion
formulas.notes: This is a key concept in trademark law

formulas.name: Intellectual Property Rights (IPRs)
formulas.formula: IPRs refer to the exclusive rights granted to creators and owners of intellectual property, such as copyrights, patents, trademarks, and industrial designs
formulas.notes: This is a key concept in intellectual property law
